"Ah!" you heard the yell of the master and looked back. Hugo had pulled himself free of the giant human's grip and, after a moment's hesitation, started tearing towards you! You looked straight ahead and committed to the sprint, but the car was surely too far away at the pace Hugo was closing in.

There was a drain cover to your left, though. Diving in could be your only chance to avoid being dog excrement.
155332211111111212221.title: The dog saw and tried to chase you

choices: Thrown out of a pipe and into the ocean!
Deposited in the sewer itself...now what?
1553322111111112122211.content:
Consumed by total panic and terror, you took a spur of the moment decision. The dog was getting way too close and you didn't think you had a chance of making the car. So you decided to do the only thing you could to effort the terrifying monster - hop into the drain.

You hurled yourself in recklessly with a degree of pain, banging off the side of the metal and falling deep into the deep, dark pit before landing with a splash in the stinking water. You struggled to keep afloat and tried to look up to see if Hugo was still there, looking for you, but before you knew it the current had taken you. You had no idea where you were going, and it was all you could do to avoid drowning!

Where do you end up?
1553322111111112122211.title: Hop into the drain!

choices: Go back the way you came
Go forward and find another way out of the sewer.
15533221111111121222112.content:
You had absolutely no idea where you were. You'd splashed to the surface of the filthy water but now a flood of water was taking you every which way. It was almost completely dark, you had no idea which way was up, and you had no idea if your head was even above water. You shut your eyes, accepting that it was completely useless to resist this current, and prayed this would be over soon. It was deeply unpleasant - the smell, the feel, the random objects that hit you...you didn't even want to imagine what they were.

After a few minutes, you struck something hard and metallic. Then, shortly after that, the chaos stopped. You realized you weren't being dragged along anymore and the water was more calm. So you opened one eye a crack and looked around. You could see a light up above! Letting the last gasp of air out of your mouth, you made for the surface and broke the water, coughing and spluttering.

The sewer. You were in the sewer. It utterly stank, so much so that you gagged before breathing through your mouth. Looking around, you noticed a set of stairs leading into the water and you swam over to it, using the well-worn, chipped concrete to climb your way out. You fell onto your back, completely exhausted, trying to get your breath.

About 20 minutes later, you stood up and climbed another step, then another, until after 5 or 6 you were at the top. You looked around. Off in the distance were some...bars, almost like a prison, in the middle of the sewer canal. There were floor-to-ceiling and covered by a concrete arch. That must have been the metallic thing you struck on the way down, so that was the way you came from. You could back that way, try and find a way up that might be somewhere roughly similar to where you started, or head in the opposite direction and look for another way out.
15533221111111121222112.title: Deposited in the sewer itself...now what?

choices: Uh-oh. The rats. They're right here.
A door of some sort...
155332211111111212221122.content:
You decided that you'd simply gone too far to make it back to where you were, so trying to get back that way was simply a fool's errand. You decided instead to head in the opposite direction, hoping you'd find any kind of exit or relief from the foul smell that enveloped you.

Soon enough the metal bars were out of sight. The walk continued almost endlessly. It felt like it had been 30 minutes and there had been little change in the scenery... still the same long, drab, vile corridor, with the daunting sounds of rats nearby and the rushing of water. You were thinking of taking a break as you rounded one last corner to see...
155332211111111212221122.title: Go forward and find another way out of the sewer.

choices: A break or meeting room for the sewer workers.
A gang den!
1553322111111112122211222.content:
You were about to sit down by the wall for a bit. You needed a break because of the sheer mind-numbing tedium of this disgusting walk, when you saw something new. A blue/grey door. Above it was a harsh strip-light, making it stand out even more.

Raising your eyebrows, you decided to head inside. Who knows what might be in there? You walked up to the door and listened. Nothing. So you got down on your belly and crawled through the crack. On the other side was...
1553322111111112122211222.title: A door of some sort...

choices: Occupied
Unoccupied
15533221111111121222112221.content:
Inside the room was a gigantic steel table, some folding chairs, and a counter with a coffee machine. There was also a whiteboard which showed various shifts and other information for the sewer workers. And what looked like a bathroom with a shower. Definitely useful.

What's more, at the far end of the room was an elevator! Would it take you out? You wondered.

But these thoughts were not your first. The first thing you noticed was that the room was...
15533221111111121222112221.title: A break or meeting room for the sewer workers.

choices: Wait by the elevator
Go and get a wash!
155332211111111212221122212.content:
Seeing the room was unoccupied, you weren't sure if that was a good thing or not. On the one hand, it meant you didn't have to worry about any...opportunistic giants snatching you up, but on the other it robbed you of the chance to hitch a ride on one of them out of the sewer. You knew you had very little chance of getting up a ladder or something so you'd likely need some human assistance.

The good news was this break room was certainly not abandoned. The coffee machine had a filter in it and in general it didn't seem particularly dusty or anything. The room had seen use, and recently too. And, best of all - there was a big pile of cleaning products, boxes of tissue, coffee filters, etc that looked easily climbable.

So now you knew the room was empty you had a choice. You could definitely climb up those boxes and get under the sink to take a much-needed bath/shower, or go and sit by the elevator waiting to jump into it as soon as it opened. Though you had no idea where it might take you, or if it was even in regular use.
155332211111111212221122212.title: Unoccupied

choices: The door
The elevator - time to run!
1553322111111112122211222121.content:
You decided getting out of the sewer was the more pressing matter for now, and that elevator looked like the best bet. Preparing yourself for a long wait, you trekked over to the other side of the room. You grabbed a tissue from the pile of boxes on the way, dragging it along with you.

By the time you made the imposing, grey elevator door, you were exhausted. It had been quite the morning - escaping a potentially tyrannical giant, a desperate chase involving another potentially tyrannical giant and a crazed dog, a rough ride through a sewer system and a trek through the drains... suffice to say you were exhausted. Using the tissue as a blanket, you huddled into a corner next to the elevator and got some sleep. You knew that the sheer noise of anyone entering the room would wake you.

And, sure enough, a couple of hours later that's exactly what happened. But where had this person come from?
1553322111111112122211222121.title: Wait by the elevator

choices: Yes - one of the men saw movement at his feet. Uh-oh!
No - safely inside the elevator.
15533221111111121222112221212.content:
You were woken with a start by the noise of the elevator in motion, and realised this was the moment. Standing up, you crept closer to the elevator door, pressing tight against the wall and peering around the corner. You were confident you wouldn't be seen at this angle.

Sure enough, the elevator doors opened up a few minutes later with a "ding". Out poured not just one man but two more, talking loudly and boisterously, all wearing high-vis jackets and hard hats. You had expected odor but there was none; they actually smelt quite good. Must be the start of the shift, not the end.

However, you were determined to get out of the sewer, interesting as these men might be. Once they walked past you, you sprinted through the elevator door, hurling yourself across the gap and landing safely inside!

But had you been spotted?
15533221111111121222112221212.title: The elevator - time to run!

choices: Pocketed and kept a secret
He shares his discovery with his two colleagues
155332211111111212221122212121.content:
Just as you got inside the elevator, you heard a "huh?" and you blanched. Oh shit. You cowered in the corner of the elevator as a massive arm reached in to stop the door from closing. Then, one of the giants walked in. A massive, hairy arm and a well-built man you guessed must have been around 40, stepped in. His handsome face looked around at the floor and you could see a beard flecked with grey.
"What's up?" came a voice.
"Nothing, I-" came the reply from this giant, cut off mid-sentence by the fact he'd seen you. You whimpered. Not good. You curled up into a ball as the man crouched down on his haunches and reached out for you slowly, wrapping his hand around you and picking you up. You knew you were trapped in his large, meaty fist but didn't want to look. Not again...

He raised his arm to his face and held it up. You chanced a look - his huge brown eyes were boring a hole right into you and you shut them again. "Whoa," came the quietly awed voice.
155332211111111212221122212121.title: Yes - one of the men saw movement at his feet.

choices: Taken with you on shift
Trapped somewhere in the locker so he could deal with you later
1553322111111112122211222121211.content:
Before you could do or say anything, the huge meaty paw of the giant had wrapped his hand around you. You could do nothing to resist as you were put inside the pocket of the giant's high-vis jacket.

"What did you see?" came a gruff voice from nearby.
"Nothing," said your giant quickly. You blanched. You didn't think him keeping you a secret was a good thing, though you supposed it could be.

You could see through the material of the jacket. He was heading towards the lockers. What was he planning, you wondered?
1553322111111112122211222121211.title: Pocketed and kept a secret

choices: No escape! Nap, so you're ready when he returns and can look for escape then
Try to escape now!
15533221111111121222112221212112.content:
Unable to protest, you were quickly grabbed out of the pocket and placed on the locker's top shelf, next to a towering bottle of pills. Next, the giant took out the bottle, opened it, and emptied the contents into another bottle. "You can go in there," he said thoughtfully, more to himself than you, "and I'll have a look at you later. What ARE you?" he added with a thoughtful interest that made you shudder. Though you supposed part of you liked the attention. Regardless, he pinched you again effortlessly and put you in the pill bottle, sealing the lid back on before you could do anything. "Hmm," you thought you heard him say. It was hot and claustrophobic in there and you really didn't enjoy the pitch black. Suddenly, a loud ripping of tape came from nearby and you blanched. The bottle was shaken around and you thought you felt the heat increase ever so slightly - he was grabbing it! You looked up at the lid and it seemed to be closing more tightly - he was taping it closed. Then you and the bottle were placed back on top with a clang.

"And I'll see you later," came the voice, which you found extremely sinister. It sent a shiver up your spine...and that wasn't helped by the locker door shutting, trapping you in even greater darkness - which you didn't even think was possible. But what could you do? Surely there was no way out of this.
15533221111111121222112221212112.title: Trapped in the locker

choices: Play dumb
Communicate with them.
1553322111111112122211222121212.content:
You were being carried roughly back away from the elevator, you knew, but you didn't know where until light flooded your vision again and you ended up on the break room table.

"Guys," boomed a voice very close by. "Come look at this!" You turned as you saw the two other giants approach the table. They were all in their 40s; the one who found you was heavily bearded, while the second guy looked to be mixed-race with brown eyes and a goatee, and the third was slightly chubbier than both but far, far taller, topping 6'5'', you estimated. He was a sight to behold. All three of them leaned in to look at you.

"You don't half find some weird stuff down here," said the guy with the goatee.
"This is the weirdest one yet," said the guy who found you.
"Definitely," grunted the tall one. "What IS it?"

You tried not to talk. Maybe you could play off as some kind of dumb animal.
"Looks like a tiny person," said the guy who found you. "Jesus..."
"Could a person really be this small?" asked goatee guy.
"Well he's right fucking there, Charlie," said the tall one roughly. "So yeah, obviously you can."

You couldn't help but since at the aggressive tone of the tall giant and this was noticed by the sharp-eyed giant who found you. "Sssh!" he said urgently, hushing the other two.

"You understand us, don't you?" he asked gently. Do you speak up?
1553322111111112122211222121212.title: He shares his discovery with his two colleagues

choices: "This is so cool. He's ours/mine." [Readers choice]
"Don't worry, we won't hurt you."
15533221111111121222112221212122.content:
You frowned, still terrified but calming slightly. It seemed the game was up.
"Um...yeah," you said. "Please don't hurt me." You figured if you lied about your invincibility and claimed you were innocent and fragile they might have a bit more sympathy for you.

All three of them gasped at once. "The hell?!" cried Charlie, the mixed-race guy with the goatee.

"Wow," breathed the guy who found you. The tall one just exhaled in disbelief.

There was a moment of silence as they considered the implications of their find.

"Jesus christ, this is amazing," said Charlie, breaking the silence temporarily, still not comprehending what he was seeing.

After another moment, the guy who found you spoke up.
15533221111111121222112221212122.title: Communicate with them.

choices: Ask them to leave you in the break room until they come back.
Ask them to take you on shift.
155332211111111212221122212121222.content:
You breathed a little sigh of relief at the words. Maybe this wouldn't be so bad. You noticed Charlie, too, had a kind and reassuring look on his face but the tall guy didn't; he just nodded.

"He looks filthy," said Charlie. "Looks like he's been through it."
It was true, you had been through a lot today - spending the night with Tim Dantry, being chased through the street by a dog and then falling down a drain - and you can't imagine you looked too great. Still, they recognised you as a person, albeit a small one.
"You could say that," you spoke up as you relaxed a little. "I fell down the drain earlier. Lucky to be alive," you added quickly, still worried about giving away your invincibility.

"Jesus. You ARE lucky. Miracle you didn't drown," boomed the tallest one suddenly. "Can't believe I'm talking to a little person." He shook his great head. "A REALLY little person. You're like, what, an inch?" his voice was full of bass and boomed around the room.
"About that," you called up to him. "I'm Rick, by the way."
"Dave. Nice to meet you, kid," said the guy who found you. "Sorry for just grabbing you like that."
"It's OK, I'm kind of used to it," you said.
"Jeff," said the tallest guy.
"Charlie," added Charlie redundantly. You nodded and gave a little wave. You saw Dave's eyebrows crease together and he gave a small, protective smile, as if he found you cute.

"How old are you, anyway?" Charlie asked.
"18," you replied automatically.
"Huh, I've got a son that age," said Dave with a smile. "Maybe I'll introduce you."

You couldn't help but warm to these guys. Maybe not Jeff so much but Dave and Charlie were being incredibly nice.

There was another awkward silence.
"So...what now?" Charlie said.
"Well," said Jeff. "I hate to break up the meet and greet but we've got work to do. Driver will be bitching at us if we haven't done the inspection by 10."

"What about the little guy?" Dave asked, and you noticed his hand drifting ever so slightly towards you as if he longed to pick you up again. "I don't wanna just leave him here, he might get caught. Or found by someone else."
"I'm OK," you protested. "I got this far, right?"
"We could take him with us," said Jeff, smirking. "We've got enough pockets." These guys were pretty handsome, you supposed you wouldn't mind. Though the concept of going back into the sewer was unpleasant.

"We're not 'taking' him anywhere. That's called kidnapping," said Charlie softly, and your heart lifted a little.
Jeff rolled his eyes. "You know what I mean," he said tersely. "And if we leave him here, he might run off."
"I won't!" you shouted desperately. And you might not even be lying, at least two of these guys seemed pretty cool.
"And so what if he does?" Dave said. "We can't stop him leaving if he wants to. Not sure how he'd work the elevator though," he added with a grin and a wink down at you.
"Thanks," you called up to Dave and Charlie. "You guys seem pretty cool and I've had a rotten morning. I'm not looking to get into any more trouble today."

Jeff shrugged, enormous, mountainous shoulders moving up to a muscular neck. "Alright then," he said. "Guess it's up to you."
155332211111111212221122212121222.title: "Don't worry, we won't hurt you."

choices: The sink - you want to get cleaned up!
A locker.
1553322111111112122211222121212221.content:
"You could leave me here," you suggested. "I'll be fine. I really don't want to go back in that sewer as long as I live. No offence," you added hastily.
Charlie chuckled. "None taken."
"No worries then," said Dave. "We'll only be an hour anyway. Where do you want us to leave you?"

You thought about it.
1553322111111112122211222121212221.title: Ask them to leave you in the break room

choices: They were nice, but you didn't want to be taken home by them. Especially Jeff!
Dive into the bath and wait for them!
15533221111111121222112221212122211.content:
"Could you please take me over to the sink? If it's not too much trouble?" you said, laying the politeness on as thick as you could. You saw Charlie's expression soften and you could tell he already liked you.
"Sure, you look like you could get cleaned up," replied Dave, reaching his huge, meaty hand towards you. But Jeff got there first, as his hand shot onto the table you were standing on. His hand was even bigger than Dave's and you fell back, but Jeff had pinched you gently - much gentler than you would have expected - in his thumb and forefinger before you could even cry out. You felt the warmth of his hand and looked up, noticing the heavily bearded giant's eyes were bugging out of his head.
"Whoa," he said deeply.
"Yeah. I know what you mean," came Dave's rough voice from nearby.

You thought Charlie was going to say something, but he seemed to stay silent on purpose. Perhaps he wanted to hold you as well. Regardless, you were quickly moved to the break room's sink and placed next to it. Jeff turned on the tap and the warm water roared into the sink. Next he squirted some nearby hand soap into it and plugged it in. Dave and Charlie appeared next to him, watching you intently. It was a little creepy but they were clearly just very interested and intrigued by you, and they seemed harmless enough.
"OK," Dave said. "We've gotta go. Enjoy your...umm...bath in the sink. Christ, this is weird," he said with a laugh that quickly became a cough. Charlie joined in.
"Very weird. Don't go running off, alright?"
"Nah," you called up to them. "You guys seem alright. Besides, Jeff is pretty scary, I don't want to piss him off."
All three of them laughed at this, Jeff slightly begrudgingly.

"You big fucking grumpy viking," said Dave, shoving Jeff lightly. "He's gonna run off because of you."
"Don't," Jeff said to you in a deep, booming, loud voice, much moreso than usual. It was so effortlessly commanding, so...alpha, somehow, you flinched. Then he broke into a smirk and he laughed. You did too. He was a terrifying man, but at least the ice was broken with him a little.

"Alright little man. See you later," said Dave. "We gotta go."

Charlie was the last to leave, giving you a happy little wave. You could hear their excited voices talking about you as they walked down the sewer corridor together for whatever work they were about to do.

You laughed, shook your head, and thought about what to do next.
15533221111111121222112221212122211.title: The sink - you want to get cleaned up!

choices: Go back out into the sewer and stay away from them
Hide somewhere in the room or wait for the elevator again
155332211111111212221122212121222111.content:
You'd gotten lucky with these guys; they had been kind enough to leave you for now. But you didn't feel particularly enthused by the idea of being taken home by one of them. And you didn't trust that they wouldn't be a bit less...kind when alone with no friends around to judge them.

You figured you had an hour so you took a quick dip in the bath they'd run for you to clean up a little, then hopped out and dried before taking a deep breath and leaping off the counter. You landed hard, but felt almost no pain due to your trusty invincibility.

Stretching, you figured you had about 45 minutes before they came back. What was the best way to evade them?
155332211111111212221122212121222111.title: Try to make a run for it

choices: Wait by the elevator; try to press the button?
Hide in the cupboards of the room.
1553322111111112122211222121212221112.content:
If you only had 45 minutes, then the chances of somebody else coming down in the elevator seemed somewhat remote. That said, it was your fastest way not just away from these guys but out of these nightmare sewers. And you'd never actually tried to press the button before - maybe you could climb up somehow.

The alternative was to hide somewhere until they left, and then try the elevator again another time - or even unknowingly hitch a ride back up with them.
1553322111111112122211222121212221112.title: Hide in the room or wait for the elevator

choices: Just some cleaning products
Empty, except for a (heavily occupied) rat hole at the back!
15533221111111121222112221212122211122.content:
You decided your best bet would be to get inside one of the cupboards, perhaps the one under the sink. You examined it - it wasn't very high up off the ground, but high enough that you'd have to jump, open it, and grab the bottom. Not a viable option. Frowning, you looked around until you saw what you wanted on the other side of the table you'd been placed on by Dave - a cupboard where the hinge wasn't quite working and the door wasn't quite flush to the counter. Perfect. You ran underneath the slightly-ajar cupboard door and leapt up, grabbing onto the floor of the cupboard and hauling yourself inside.

That bath had done wonders; you felt refreshed and way more energetic. Half an hour ago you'd been dead on your feet. You supposed you had Dave and co. to thank for that. Dusting yourself down, you took a look at what was inside the cupboard you'd just entered.
15533221111111121222112221212122211122.title: Hide in the cupboards of the room.

choices: You run out of the cupboard and reveal yourself to them.
No, leave it. Keep hiding.
155332211111111212221122212121222111221.content:
You noticed the cupboard was full of cleaning products and smelled strongly of bleach and soap. You smiled - perfect. You ran to the very back of the cupboard, through the dense forest of multi-coloured bottles, and hid behind a particularly large one. Then you lay down and took a nap on the cupboard floor, confident the noise of the three giants would wake you up when they returned.

You were woken with a start about half an hour later when the three giants returned. Holding your breath you got to your feet, pressing up against the back of the bottle. You looked around - there weren't many escape routes if they chose to empty this cupboard.

Still, you had to admit this was very exciting. The idea of these three big, hairy giants searching for you, banging around the room, excited you.

Then, a voice. "He's gone!" sounded like Dave.
"What?" Jeff.
"He's gone! He's not where we left him!"
"God damnit," came Jeff's growling voice. "I TOLD you we should have kept him with us."
"Yeah, and that still would have been kidnapping," Charlie said. "Look, let's see if we can find him. Maybe we can explain to him he's better off with us. And if he still wants to hide, then so be it."

Your heart softened a little. Charlie really was a lovely guy, it seemed. You kind of wanted to reveal yourself to them now. Or maybe just to him...or Dave. Jeff, though, terrified you.
155332211111111212221122212121222111221.title: Just some cleaning products

choices: Spotted - by all, or just one?
Not spotted - and kicked by one of them accidentally! You're sent flying!
1553322111111112122211222121212221112211.content: Screw it, you decided. You were unlikely to find nicer guys than this. You made for the exit to the cupboard and, pushing it open, crawled out. You looked up to see the three giants still standing around the bath you'd been left in, talking. You made your way over to the 6 enormous legs, hoping to get their attention.
1553322111111112122211222121212221112211.title: You run out of the cupboard and reveal yourself

choices: All 3
Just one!
1553322111111112122211222121212221112211.content: You reached the giants and called out. But did all three of them notice, or just one?
1553322111111112122211222121212221112211.title: Spotted - by all, or just one?

choices: Jeff
Charlie or Dave
1553322111111112122211222121212221112211.title: Just one!

choices: Charlie
Dave
1553322111111112122211222121212221112211.content: Which of the two kinder giants?
1553322111111112122211222121212221112211.title: Charlie or Dave

choices: They give up their search - try and hitch a ride back up with them?
Someone looks in the cupboard for you.
1553322111111112122211222121212221112212.content: You had made your choice, though. You didn't want these guys to take you and you wanted out. So you stayed hidden behind the cleaning products for now.
1553322111111112122211222121212221112212.title: No, leave it. Keep hiding.

choices: Jeff
Charlie or Dave
1553322111111112122211222121212221112212.title: Someone looks in the cupboard for you.

choices: Charlie
Dave
1553322111111112122211222121212221112212.content: Which one of the two kinder giants finds you?
1553322111111112122211222121212221112212.title: Charlie or Dave

choices: Dave moves the cleaning product out of the way and sees you!
Dave quickly gives up and you breathe a sigh of relief.
1553322111111112122211222121212221112212.content:
You panicked a little as giant footsteps moved over to your cupboard hiding place. You pressed up against the bottle of cleaning product, hoping against hope that whichever giant it was passed it by. But no, light flooded the place as the door was opened and a gruff voice said, "little man? You in here?" It echoed around the cave you were in and you recognised it as Charlie's voice. Well, at least it wasn't Jeff...

You tried to breathe as little as possible, willing yourself to disappear and cursing yourself - if you'd just stayed put these guys would have been fine with you. Instead they would now be less likely to trust you, more likely to capture you somehow.

So is the handsome, goateed giant going to find you?
1553322111111112122211222121212221112212.title: Charlie

choices: Charlie doesn't respect your wishes and tells the other two guys about you.
Charlie stays quiet
1553322111111112122211222121212221112212.content:
(Apologies for the error in the previous choices)

Before you could react, Charlie's enormous, light brown hand had grabbed the bottle above you and was moving it to the side. Turning around, you tried to move with it but he simply moved it too fast for you to grab onto. You stumbled back at the sheer force with which it was moved out of the way, and then looked up to see Charlie's goateed face light up with recognition and relief.

Dropping to your knees, you put your finger over your lips, begging for Charlie's silence.
1553322111111112122211222121212221112212.title: Charlie finds you!

choices: Yes - Charlie thinks you want to go home with just him
No - Dave is just giving you a little stroke goodbye before he lets you go.
1553322111111112122211222121212221112212.content:
Seeing your distress, Charlie gave the slightest of nods, almost imperceptible. You were relieved, and your previous good impressions of the man were confirmed. You pointed to the cleaning product and began to make your way back to it...

Suddenly, Charlie's hand began to reach out for you. You gave a cry of surprise and stumbled back again, this time falling down. You realised something - maybe Charlie had misconstrued what you were doing. Maybe he thought you wanted to be taken home, but only by him. Maybe he thought you only wanted giant, that three would be too much. Were you right?
1553322111111112122211222121212221112212.title: Charlie stays quiet

choices: Yes - do your best to get out of there at the first chance.
No - taken home by the giant Charlie!
1553322111111112122211222121212221112212.content:
Oh no, you thought, as Charlie's manly hand wrapped around you easily, closing you almost immediately in a tight fist. You thrashed around, nearly suffocated by it, but you were placed in his pocket very quickly before you had time to do anything.

"Anything?" Dave said from across the room.
"Nothing," Charlie said, as you heard the cupboard door close.

You looked around the pocket. It didn't smell great in there and you didn't particularly enjoy being so up-close-and-personal with the giant. 1553322111111112122211222121212221112212.title: Yes - Charlie thinks you want to go home with him!

1553322111111112122211222121212221112212.content:
You decided that attempting to escape the deep pocket would be futile and opted instead to conserve your energy for later in case you needed to make a quick escape. You were hoping Charlie had just misunderstood you.

After a few more minutes of searching for you, Charlie made an excuse and left the other two guys, heading to the elevator. On the way up, he stroked the pocket again and you felt the weight of his massive hand pressing down on you. After a while he got to his car and said, "we'll be home in a minute, little guy."

Great, you thought, rolling your eyes.

But he was almost literally correct. It was a very short drive back to Charlie's house, and he quickly climbed out of his car and walked into his house, grabbing you out of his pocket gently and putting you on his coffee table.

"Hey there little guy," he said very, very quietly. There was great empathy and kindness in his voice and his huge, expressive face displayed great concern for you. "Hope you're OK. So you only wanted to be found by one big guy, huh?"

He seemed so genuinely caring and you couldn't help but feel a little taken by the fact that he genuinely seemed to care. But you hadn't wanted to be taken. So did you tell him the truth?

Go back to Dave's place for a beer with Dave and Charlie
1553322111111112122211222121212221122.content:
It was a nice, chilled out hour of peace. An oasis of calm in the chaos of your life. But soon you were awoken by the noise of boots approaching the door as the three giants walked back into the break room. They immediately hurried over to you and loomed over the sink, staring into it and spying you immediately. "Wow, he's still here," boomed Jeff. You looked up and waved, confident they wouldn't be able to see the rest of your naked body through the water.

You swam to the edge of the sink and the goateed Charlie crouched down so his face was level with you, filling your vision with his handsome face. He spoke in a kind, quiet voice. "Listen, little man, we're all done for the day so we were going to go back to Dave's place for a beer. You want to come?"

You looked up past Charlie, to Dave and Jeff on either side, who - now you'd reached the end of the sink closest to you - towered over you. They were terrifying in one way, but gentle and comforting in another. And you supposed you'd already made your choice when you decided to stay in the sink. You nodded. "Sure," you called.

Charlie's face broke into a radiant smile. "Great."

Jeff checked his phone. "Ah shit," he said roughly. "I've got a meeting with the realtor now. Totally forgot."
"Oh damn," Dave said, but not altogether genuinely, you thought.
"Yeah. Well, the little guy could ride with me if he wants and I'll come over with him later."
1553322111111112122211222121212221122.title: Uneventful hour of relaxation; the guys return.

Dave doesn't bother with the shower; takes you to the kitchen to grab the beers
15533221111111121222112221212122211222.content:
Truth be told, Jeff scared you a bit and you weren't fully comfortable being alone with him. Dave and Charlie, however, were kind enough and you'd gladly enjoy a beer with them, for now. And see how it went from there.

"Um, well if I'm going to end up there anyway I might as well just go with them," you said apologetically, not wanting to anger Jeff too much.
An annoyed expression flashed across the face of the tall, heavily bearded giant, but then he shrugged again. "Sure, whatever," he said. "I'll meet you guys later."

"Nice. We'll let you get dressed," said Charlie quietly. He stood up and the three huge guys turned around, preserving your modesty as you pulled yourself out of the sink and put on your dirty clothes.
"Done!" you called.

Dave turned around and held out his hand and you hesitated before climbing into it slowly. He curled his hand around you and you watched as the huge, rough fingers surrounded your entire body, securing you in place. "You can ride in here," Dave said kindly as you suddenly found yourself sliding into a pocket on his high-vis jacket. You could see through the bright material, but not for long as Dave then put another jacket over the top of it and patted you gently. "You'll be nice and safe in there."

You heard Jeff snort in derision. "You gonna give him a wheel to run in next?"
"What?"
"He's not a little pet. He's just a small guy. Stop talking to him like he's a pet hamster."
Charlie laughed. "Shut up, Jeff."

Together the three of them walked to the elevator and got in separate cars. The ride home was uneventful, and you were soon at Dave's house. Charlie lived just three doors down the street, Dave had told you on the way as part of a one-sided conversation (you didn't want to talk too much while he was driving, he was distracted enough occasionally patting you with quivering, excited hands). He had stopped at home to shower and change.
15533221111111121222112221212122211222.title: Head to Dave and Charlie's for a beer.

You knew you were in Dave's house by the sound of it; it was peaceful, quiet, the only noise Dave's huge footsteps. You were surprised when you heard the familiar thud of someone ascending stairs; Dave was going up. After a few more footsteps you were removed from his pocket and placed on a soft, springy surface; you were in his large bedroom, which had nice, calming blue wallpaper and a huge bed. You looked around. "Nice place," you called up to him.
"Thanks, little man," he said with a smile. "Listen, it might shock you to learn this but working in the sewers makes you stink. So I'm gonna go take a shower and change. Do you mind waiting a minute?"

Dave and Charlie just want to talk
1553322111111112122211222121212221122211.content:
"Sure," you said with a shrug. "Do you think you could get something else for me to wear please? If it's not too much trouble?"
"No trouble. You're right, those clothes are filthy. I'll find you something."

Dave returned 15 minutes later, having showered, wearing jeans and a grey t-shirt. You could really see just how hairy and thick his arms were now. The beard, though not as thick or as long as Jeff's, was more prominent too. He was carrying a box and a couple of...socks. White sports socks. You looked at him quizzically.

"Don't worry. These are clean. Barely wore em," he added.

The box turned out to be a sewing kit.
"This belonged to my wife before we split up," he said.
"Oh. I'm sorry," you said genuinely.
"It's OK. Happened about 15 years ago, we're on good terms. She moved to Australia, kid lives here. More importantly for you, she left this here...now let's see..."

He put the box on the bed and pulled a chair up to it, then sat down. He was sat, legs apart, directly in front of you and you stared up at him, a little intimidated, as those hirsute arms began rooting around the box.
You were surprised. You hadn't expected Dave to have any idea what he was doing but he turned out to be pretty handy with a needle and thread. He measured you, cut up the socks and sewed it together to create several thick onesies. It was a bit of a rough fit but it worked.
"Wow," you said, wearing one. "Feels good!"
Dave looked at you and couldn't help but laugh, a deep and booming laugh. "Sorry, it's just funny watching you wearing my socks. It's so ridiculous."
"Yep..." you said.
"You are just so damn small, little guy. I don't know how you lived this long. But you're safe now."

He reached down with a finger and gently patted your head. Jeff was right, the treatment was getting a bit pet-like, but it was also very sweet and tender and you could tell Dave was being as nice as possible.

"Thanks," you said awkwardly. "Guess I'm lucky you saw me."
"Yeah. You don't even know what was on the top of that elevator, huh? You might have never gotten to safety."
You imagined either a public park or a facility or something else, but you actually didn't know. You were about to ask when you heard the doorbell ring.
"Just in time," boomed Dave, shifting his enormous weight towards you. You couldn't help but look at the crotch of his jeans as it was thrust in our direction, but suddenly the hand descended in front of you and curled around you. You were put on Dave's shoulder this time, where you sat as he carefully walked downstairs and opened the door to let Charlie in. Charlie was now wearing roughly the same as Dave - jeans and a t-shirt - and he still looked gorgeous, aging with real grace.
"Jesus, Dave, are you sure that's safe?" asked Charlie the second he saw you, concern all over his face.
"Yeah, as long as he grabs the shirt he'll be fine," Dave said. You felt the tension in his shoulders and thought maybe he wanted to shrug, but thought better of it.

Charlie stepped inside and Dave grabbed two beers, carrying them and you into the living room. He kept the cap of one of the bottles and poured a couple of drops into it, placing it down on the table for you. Then you were placed there too, the two huge bottles towering on either side of you and the two much bigger giants sitting on the sofa, both leaning forward and looking directly at you. It was still unnerving, though slightly less so.

You had never had beer before, but wanted to be respected by these older guys, so you took a swig. "Good man," Dave said with a big grin.

Leaning forward, the two 40-somethings were still watching you with burning, excited curiosity.

"Did you make clothes for him?" Charlie asked.
"Not bad, right?" replied his friend.
"Are they...did you...are those your socks?" Charlie asked, half-appalled, half-amused.
"Yeah," Dave replied with a shrug. "They were clean. BARELY worn..." you noticed Dave shift a little in his seat, awkwardly.

Charlie looked at you almost apologetically. "They're fine. Nice and warm anyway," you called up to him. You didn't want to insult Dave. After all, he'd done a pretty good job.
"See? No problem," Dave said.

"So how'd you get like this, little guy? If you don't mind me asking."
"It's OK," I said. "I was born like this. Family didn't really want me. Kept me secret. I left once I turned 18, tried to make it for myself." OK, it was a lie, but not a total one. You just didn't want these guys trying to bring you back to your brother, back to normality - you still wanted the adventure. You noticed the look of sympathy on their faces. "It's fine, honestly. I don't mind being on my own."
"And how was making it for yourself going? Before you fell into the sewer, I mean," asked Charlie.
"Not bad. I lived in a couple of houses and stole their food. It was fun, sneaking around."
"So I'm the first "giant" guy to find you?" said Dave with a cocky smirk.
You shook your head. "Sorry, no. I was looking for a new place to live, walking down the street early this morning, and this giant bastard and his stupid big dog found me. I had to jump into the sewer to escape them."
"Jesus christ," Charlie said. "What a prick."
"Exactly," you said. "But hey, it all worked out."

They both smiled down at you paternally and took a satisfied swig of beer each.
"Don't worry about Jeff," Dave said suddenly. "He may come off a bit miserable, but he's a good dude. I've known him for years. I know he was intimidating you a little bit before. I mean, hell, he intimidates me sometimes."

"You said you were looking for somewhere new to live?"

"Well no need to keep looking," Charlie said. "We've all got plenty of room."

You pulled a face, though you were a little amused by Dave's possessive nature.

"Yeah, 'course I am," replied Dave coarsely.

You waved. "Thanks, guys. Let me think about it." Truth be told you weren't that convinced about settling down so fast but maybe living with one of these guys could be an adventure in itself.

Suddenly there was the sound of a car door slamming and, shortly after, someone approaching the door. Charlie instinctively leaned forward to hide you from view.

You felt a little wave of affection for him; he was so shy and unassuming given his massive physical dominance. You shrugged. You knew a couple of giants your own age, mostly introduced by your brother, but not really very many. And it was always so careful and controlled and under your brother's careful supervision. This was different, it was yours.

"Sure," you said. "I'll meet him."

A few seconds later the front door opened and a deep but more youthful-sounding voice called out. "Dad, I'm home!"
"In here, big man," said Dave, raising his voice but not too much. He was very kindly staying conscious of you, slightly modulating his voice, you thought.

He had black hair cut short, dressed casually in jeans and a t-shirt that his shoulders and arms bulged against. His legs seemed to stretch for miles.

"Jake, this is Rick. Rick, my son Jake."
You heard a suppressed laugh in Dave's voice at the situation as well as your mutual amazement. Because Jake, too, was staring at you with intense confusion, and that was an unnerving sight.
"We found him this morning in the sewer," Charlie added quietly. "Pretty cool, huh?"
"What-" Jake said. His mouth opened and closed like a goldfish.
You got over your initial shock at Dave's height, but you supposed you were a bigger surprise. "He's tall," you said to Dave and Charlie.
"Yep. Got a basketball scholarship and everything. High hopes."
"Wow, cool," you said, as Jake recovered. He took several steps forward until he was standing right over the coffee table you were perched on.
"So...wait, you're a 1-inch man my Dad found in the sewer?"

"How-how are you-what?" Jake was still struggling to process this news.

Dave shook his head and stood up. "Look, it's been a long morning for the little guy. How about you show him upstairs and he can take a nap somewhere. And we'll grab some lunch after."

You looked around at Dave, who gave you a reassuring look. "Don't worry, my kid wouldn't hurt a fly, gangly as he looks."

Jake lowered his shaking hands and you boldly stepped onto one, then held on as Jake very carefully raised you all the way up to his face, which was so so high. He was by far the tallest person you had ever met.
"Jesus christ," Jake said under his breath. "You are so tiny."
"What else is new?" you replied cheekily, and Jake laughed.
"Alright, little man, alright, let's go." Your cheeks burned as the ice between you broke. "Back in a bit," said Jake as he carried you out of the room. You hoped Dave really knew his son.

Jake was leaving for university and you would have to decide whether to go with him, or stay with the three handsome workers who had found you and been so kind with him. You wouldn't sure what to do, and Jake had been polite about not asking about it, but Jake was due to leave in a week and you had to choose sooner or later.

One night you decided you would make your decision. It was awkward to bring it up but you owed them both. During dinner with just Dave and his enormously tall son, you said, "so I've been thinking."

"Yeah, that's dangerous. Last time you thought you nearly got washed down the sink."
You smiled and shook your head. "No, really. I think I'm going to..."

Owen broke into a broad grin and gave a sigh of relief. "I was hoping you'd say that," he said, reaching out with a finger and giving you a gentle, if slightly condescending, pat on the head. You could tell he was already developing some affection for you.
"You want to go and get cleaned up while you wait? There's a staff bathroom in the back..."

You raised your eyebrows. Yes, you could really do with getting cleaned up, but you didn't expect Owen to care so much about the miniscule amount of dirt you'd collected. "Wow. Thanks, that'd be great."
"Wow? What do you mean wow?" he replied.
"Well, I didn't expect a giant to care about me being dirty or not."
Owen blushed a little. "Well, you know. It's not healthy. Don't want you getting sick. Here," he said, sticking out a palm flat on the table. You moved towards it but before you could do anything you were swept into it by his other massive hand. The long fingers curled around you to keep you from falling as you were carried to the staff bathroom. He disinfected the sink, filled it up and added soap. He left you on the side of the sink. "Here. Nobody else will need to use this room for an hour, so I'll be back then."
"Are you sure?" you asked.
"Why? You gonna run off?" he said with a smile.
You laughed. You liked this guy. "No, I'm not going anywhere."
"Good," he replied with a grin and walked away, a spring in his step. You could tell he was really enjoying his tiny find.

You smiled at him, happy with the choice you'd made to introduce yourself to him, and took off your ragged clothes before jumping head first into the sink.

The warm, soapy water was relaxing and just what you needed. You bathed in it, swam in it, and generally relaxed in it. Propping yourself up in the corner, you drifted off slowly to sleep.
1553322111111112122212121122211122.title: Head home with Owen when his shift is done.

choices: Owen being stopped by the police!
Owen arriving at his small apartment.
15533221111111121222121211222111221.content:
You were awoken by the sound of Owen crashing through the door. You were aware that he had just walked through it normally, but to you the sound was cacophonous as always.
"I'm done, little dude. You ready?"
"Yeah," you called sleepily. "Just give me a minute."
"Sure."

You clambered up out of the sink and turned on the tap, which was a struggle. You then climbed back in, rinsing off in the cold water, before climbing back out again to dry using the roll of tissue he had left behind for you. Then you got dressed. "Ready!" you called. Owen stomped across the room, his movement threatening to shake you back into the sink, but you held fast. You were used to keeping your balance by now. You stared up, all the way up, at the giant.
"Alright, we're heading to my place then," he said, a note of excitement in his voice. "You're sure you want to come?"
You couldn't believe you had a choice. What a good dude. "Why not?" You were excited by this new adventure in your life. And Owen couldn't seem to believe the carefree attitude you had towards this. He grinned widely and held out his hand, which you hopped into.
He brought it up, elevator-style, to his face. "You'll be safe. Promise."
It was so cute, him not knowing of your invincibility. "Alright," you said solemnly, trying so hard not to laugh and give the game away. He lowered the hand down to his pants pocket and carefully tipped you inside before patting it gently.
"We'll be home in about 15 minutes. Hope it's comfy in there," came his voice, reverberating through the body.

And it was, though it smelt a bit weird. Pockets tended to smell weird, you found. Either way, you drifted off back to sleep while you were in there, awoken by the sound of his car starting and by the occasional pat on the pocket, as if to check you were still there.


You were woken up by...
15533221111111121222121211222111221.title: An hour later, Owen returns...

choices: Continue...
Time-skip to late that night, with them about to go to bed
155332211111111212221212112221112212.content:
You were jolted fully awake when Owen's hand once again invaded the pocket and pulled you out, bringing you once again to his colossal face. "Oh good, you're ok," he said. "Were you sleeping?" he said, noticing your bleariness.
"Yeah," you said. "It was comfy in there."
Owen laughed. "Cool. Good to know. I'll put you in there again then sometime."
He transferred you from his fingers to his palm and held it out for you to look around. The bedroom was - by a giant's standard - fairly small, with room for a single bed, a wardrobe, a desk, a chair and not much else. There were a couple of band posters on the wall but that was it. "It's not much," he said. "But it's big enough for you, I guess," he added with a half-smile.
You rolled your eyes at the obligatory height joke. "Yeah, I could probably sleep somewhere here," you said.
"There's always the living room, but it gets kind of cold at night. I could help you better if you were here too."
You had to stop him here. "I don't need your help, dude."

The hand you were on contracted, the fingers closing in on you ever so slightly. Maybe he was wincing. "Sorry, I didn't - I didn't mean anything by it. I just meant-"
"It's fine," you said, cutting him off. Not knowing about your invincibility was one thing, but you didn't want him thinking you were some helpless insect. "I'm just-I'm not, like, weak. I fed myself for a long time before I met you."
"OK, sure. Sorry. Do you want to stay in here then? I promise not to help you too much," he said with a nervous chuckle.
"Good," you replied. "Sure."
"Awesome. This is going to be so cool. Can't believe I have an actual real-life little tiny person living with me. I get pretty lonely in here sometimes, it'll be nice to have some company."

As long as Owen stayed appreciative of you, you were happy to stay.
155332211111111212221212112221112212.title: Owen arriving at his small apartment.

choices: You turn in too - timeskip to next morning/a few weeks later?
Can't sleep. Go and explore the apartment.
1553322111111112122212121122211122122.content:
After setting up your lodgings and watching as Owen made dinner - well, he reheated some curry and gave you a little bit of the chicken - it was time to sleep. He'd set you up under his own bed, laying out one of his old hoodies and some clean socks for you to sleep in. It was better than anything Tim Dantry had done, anyway. And he said if it was a problem, he could find something else. But you figured it'd be fine.

"Alright," said Owen, coming in from the bathroom wearing just a plain white t-shirt and loose boxer shorts. "I'm gonna turn in, little guy. You alright?"
"Yeah," you called, looking up, all the way up, at him. His legs were hairier than you might have thought, and a bit more muscular. "Thanks for setting this up," you added.
"Dude, it's no problem," he replied. He turned off the light to the bedroom, leaving only the bedside light on, then took a step closer towards you, phone in hand. Now you had to crane your neck even more. "You just shout up to me if you need anything."
You were sceptical if you'd be able to wake him up, but you decided to humour him. "Sure," you said.
"Alright then." Carefully, the giant stepped right over you - an astonishing, humbling sight that left your mouth open - and climbed into bed.
"Night, little man," he said with a grin. "Sleep well." You smiled back at him. This was going very well.
1553322111111112122212121122211122122.title: Time to sleep

choices: Owen notices. Time for a talk.
Owen doesn't notice - continue.
15533221111111121222121211222111221221.content:
You woke up in your little bed and stretched out expansively. You felt lazy, content, as you realised what had broken you up - Owen's snores. You rolled your eyes but then gave an affectionate smile. He was cute. Truth be told you'd grown very fond of the giant over the last few weeks. He was certainly very kind to you. You rolled over and tried to get back to sleep but it was useless.

Stretching out, you got out of your sock-bed and moved out from under the bed. Your clothes were nearby and you were only wearing a pair of crudely-made shorts.

Suddenly, without warning, Owen's two huge bare feet landed on either side of you. Their incredible impact, which gave you no time to brace, threw you to the ground. You yelled out in surprise as you fell, landing on your back and staring up, winded. The huge feet became huge, hairy calves which became huge, hairy legs, which became a pair of loose boxers and eventually, Owen himself. You hadn't told him yet about the crush you had on him.

He'd heard your cry of anguish and looked down, his eyes adjusting quickly and finding you.

"Oh shit," he said. "Sorry, little man."
You coughed, still recovering. Bending down, the handsome giant plucked you off the ground with his thumb and index finger, which enveloped almost your whole body. He wasn't as careful as he used to be, but you didn't really care. You were held up to his face, and, wanting to avoid falling out, you stayed still.
"Ow," you spluttered, pinched fast between the two digits.
"That was a close one!" Owen said. He still didn't know about your invincibility.

You stared into his brown eyes deeply.
"My fault," I said. "Shoulda waited."
"It's OK," he said, "just try to be more careful. Don't know what I'd do without my little guy," he said with a mischievous grin.
You smiled. "You won't have to worry about that."

Suddenly, the gigantic Owen threw you into the air, high above his head, before he caught you quickly in two cupped hands. "Cool," he said. You were used to being thrown by now and just laughed. Landing in the hands, Owen started playing with your tiny body with one finger. You squirmed beneath the finger which seemed to tickle your entire body, and slowly began to get hard as the giant's booming laugh reverberated around you.
15533221111111121222121211222111221221.title: A few weeks later...

choices: Owen is happy about this development.
Owen is grossed out and got rid of you.
155332211111111212221212112221112212211.content:
Suddenly, Owen stopped playing with you and moved his hand away. You moved your hands to your shorts to adjust but it was too late. You were tenting them and it was hard to conceal even at your small size.

"I see you're enjoying yourself," Owen half-joked, a little nervousness in his voice.
You coughed and looked away. "Um...yeah..."
"You got a crush on me, tiny man?" he said, still in that same half-joking, nervous tone.

You kept looking away, not wanting to say anything, but the silence was your answer.

"Huh," Owen said thoughtfully.

"Sorry," you said.
155332211111111212221212112221112212211.title: Owen notices. Time for a talk.

choices: Tell him about your invincibility
See what he does first
1553322111111112122212121122211122122111.content:
Owen broke into a genuine smile. "What are you sorry for? I've been into you from the moment we met!"
You blinked. Surely you'd misheard. "What?!"
"Dude, I didn't know it before, but having all that power was a real turn-on for me."

He excitedly sat down on his bed and stared at you in a new, excited way. "Are you serious?" you asked, excited.
"Yeah, man. I guess I have a thing for being a giant. I didn't want to act on it though because I like having you around and I thought it might creep you out."

"Wow," you said with a smile. "That's so great. I guess it would have been easy for a giant like you to take advantage."

Owen's eyes lit up upon being called a "giant". You'd never noticed before but it was evidently a turn-on for him.

"Yeah, I guess. It was hard not to for a while there, but I calmed down once I got to know you a bit better."

He lay down on his bed, propping up his head on his pillow and placing you on his bare chest. You stared around the muscular body in awe. "Wow," you said.
"You like it?"
"Yeah," you said.

You had a feeling things were about to escalate.
1553322111111112122212121122211122122111.title: Owen is happy about this development.

choices: "Time to go back to work." [Return to the gas station.]
"Got a day off. What do you want to do?"
155332211111111212221212112221112212212.content:
Thankfully for you, Owen hadn't noticed what was happening. You breathed a sigh of relief, though you knew that if you kept staying with him you'd have to talk about it eventually.

Still dressed only in his boxers, Owen carried you to the kitchen and made breakfast, where he fed you some. Then he took you back to the bedroom, showered - man, you wished you'd been able to look - and came back into the room in a fresh pair of boxers. He stretched out and smiled. "Alright..." he said.
155332211111111212221212112221112212212.title: Owen doesn't notice - continue.

choices: Day inside - pizza and videogames
Head out somewhere
1553322111111112122212121122211122122122.content: You shrugged. Your days off had been pretty fun so far, but Owen had always been the one to think of whatever you'd be doing. It was weird to have the ball in your court.
1553322111111112122212121122211122122122.title: "Got a day off. What do you want to do?"

choices: Leave the apartment - not for good, just to see what the hall looked like
Head into the kitchen. Could you make food for yourself?
15533221111111121222121211222111221222.content:
You tossed and turned in bed, trying to get to sleep, but it was eluding you. You decided you would explore the empty apartment; you figured you might be here for a while and wanted to get a feel for the place.

You got up, stretched, and left the room, easily crawling under the door. What did you want to do?
15533221111111121222121211222111221222.title: Can't sleep. Go and explore the apartment.

choices: Yes. (New character is readers' choice)
No.
155332211111111212221212112221112212221.content:
If you were going to stay here, you decided it was better to get your bearings a bit. You rolled out of bed and began a slow walk to the front door, then crawled underneath it, walking a few paces out into the corridor.

It was fairly shabby, but not a hovel or anything like that. You could hear footsteps in some of the other apartments and from the floor above. You were about to go back in when someone exited the apartment directly opposite Owen's. You gave a yelp and retreated to the door. Did the new man see you?
155332211111111212221212112221112212221.title: Leave the apartment - not for good, just to see

choices: Seen
Not seen
1553322111111112122212121122212.content:
You decided to see what he did if he had the control, the power. So you decided to sneak behind the counter and knock something over "accidentally".

You quickly found your goal - a can of insect repellent. With all your might, you took a run at the thing and knocked it to the ground with a loud clatter, your heart beating rapidly. "Huh?" said the cashier as, suddenly, his huge face appeared underneath the counter. "What the-" he said suddenly, as his eyes locked onto the can. But does he see you or are you going to have to try something else?
1553322111111112122212121122212.title: Let him "accidentally" find you.

choices: The giant likes having the upper hand and controls you
Giant just wants to talk
15533221111111121222121211222121.content:
The eyes searched furtively under the counter, the handsome face looking thoughtful, quizzical.

"Whoa!" he said suddenly as the eyes locked unmistakably onto you. His hand shot out and grabbed you, pulling you up and onto the counter with nauseating speed. You didn't even have time to have second thoughts. Before you knew it you were trapped inside cupped hands, staring up at your giant "captor".

"What the hell," said the giant quietly. "What are you? Are you a person?"
"...Yes..." you added, and you did not have to fake how scared you were.
15533221111111121222121211222121.title: Seen

choices: Stay in this guy's luggage. Who knew where he was headed?
Once you're at the airport, get out of the bag and catch a different flight.
155332211111111212221212112222.content:
After waiting an hour, eventually someone else showed up. It was a pretty average-looking guy with black hair and a little bit of stubble. He was wearing a t-shirt and three-quarter length trousers, and shades.

You listened into the conversation he had with the cashier.

"Nice ride," said the cashier. He sounded pretty young.
"Only a rental, sadly," said the new guy, and there was a little bit of regret in his voice.
"Oh?"
"Yeah, I'm taking it back to the airport now. Flying home later."
"Ah...well, enjoy it while it lasts!"

Your eyes widened. The airport! If you wanted an adventure there'd be no better place to go - because you could go anywhere you wanted! As the giants completed their transaction, you sprinted back across the baking forecourt and to the giant's car. It was indeed a sweet ride - a blue convertible. The giant was on the way back, and you decided to use him to your advantage. One of his trainers had a dangling shoelace, which you grabbed onto for a second as he climbed into the car. Before you knew it, you were in the car's footwell and scrambling under the seat. Safe from prying eyes. You climbed through as the car started and came out the other side of the seat. On the back seat was the giant's carry-on luggage, so with the giant speeding down the road to the airport you unzipped the bag and forced yourself into a side pocket.

Time to head to the airport.
155332211111111212221212112222.title: A tourist arrives

choices: Domestic flight
International flight
1553322111111112122212121122222.content:
After a while, and some chaotic movement, you heard frantic footsteps, noise, and announcements made over a tannoy. You were at the airport!

You didn't particularly want to go through an x-ray so you decided to try to clamber out of the bag once it stopped. Sure enough, the man you'd stowed away with was soon joining a bag-drop queue. It was busy but you figured nobody would look at the floor too hard, especially with lots of trolleys and stuff to hide under.

So you crawled out, and immediately you were assaulted by the cacophony of footsteps and noise. You'd never been anywhere this crowded before!

Spotting a gap in the crowd of people, you sprinted away from the bag-drop queue and under some nearby benches, breathing a sigh of relief. There was a couple sitting directly above you, but you paid them no heed for now. Instead, you crept up against the side of the bench to give yourself as much cover as possible before peering out, looking for the departure board.

Soon enough, you found it. So where did you want to go?
1553322111111112122212121122222.title: At the airport!

choices: Europe
Other
15533221111111121222121211222222.content:
You decided to broaden your horizons as much as possible and get out of the good old US of A. Time to head abroad!
Where to?

choices: He sees you and is able to pull you out of there.
You evade the light and get out of the tyre!
15533221111111121222121212.content:
You leapt into the tyre with reckless abandon, smacking your head on the metal as you went.

Woozily, you got up, only to hear a rough voice saying "that was a little person!" in a state of shock. Uh-oh. He was on to you.

You weren't sure how to proceed - better to let this guy make the first move. You heard and felt pounding footsteps as they approached the tyre on the drivers' side door. "I know you're in there, little man," said a sinister voice. "Or woman," it added. You rolled your eyes. But now you knew where he was - the door side. You could simply sneak out the other side and-

Bright light burned through your thoughts as the inside of the tyre was lit up like the inside of a disco ball. You shielded your eyes as the flashlight from Hugo's master's phone shone through the darkness, searching for you!
15533221111111121222121212.title: No

choices: The owner of the car arrives and joins the hunt!
The dog owner gets bored and leaves
155332211111111212221212122.content:
Panicking, you scrambled out of the tyre, away from the light. You were glad to evade it, but you weren't out of trouble yet. You sprinted across the front of the car to the other tyre, climbing into that one too and praying you'd evaded that awful dog and his apparently equally awful owner.

Instead, you heard a low laugh. "I know you're here," he said. "You'll have to come out eventually."

But the voice was still far away, by the first tyre. Maybe you'd successfully evaded him!
155332211111111212221212122.title: You evade the light and get out of the tyre!

choices: Jump in the hole!
Try and find somewhere else in the kitchen to hide!
15533221111111122.content:
You decide not to risk it. Waiting here is still better than waiting next to the front door and hoping Tim opens it long enough to slip out. That draught was definitely coming from somewhere down there. So you sat at the edge of the void, legs dangling, waiting nervously.

You'd never been out of the sight of your protectors before, so this was a whole new experience for you. You have to admit, it was exciting! Maybe you should have taken more risks before. After all, you were invincible. But, you thought, that could work against you if you were captured. Tim, for instance, could make your life a total hell with no real consequences. Still, once you got safe you'd talk to your brother and his friends in the door about living a bit more freely.

Suddenly, though, the fear that you'd experienced a lot in the last 24 hours returned as you heard thunderous movements from upstairs. Tim was out of bed, stumbling around...the blood left your face. You hoped against hope that he'd just go back to bed, as the ceiling above you seemed to shake with his footsteps, but then you heard the rumble of his voice. "Little man?" he called out. "Little man?" his voice rose with something like concern. You screwed up your face. Maybe escaping wasn't the best idea...but you were pretty set on it for now, so you put that idea out of your mind.

Suddenly the footsteps grew louder and louder, more pronounced, more terrifying. Tim was approaching the stairs. You looked down into the hole. It was still too dark to see anything! But it might be the only way to escape the behemoth.
15533221111111122.title: Be patient and wait until morning!

choices: Tim keeps looking for you downstairs - does he find you?
Tim gets ready to leave for work - time to make your escape!
155332211111112.content:
You decide that your best bet will be to simply wait until Tim opens the door in the morning and then slip out. There's a shoe-rack near the door, upon which are several different pairs of shoes - smart dress shoes, a couple of pairs of trainers, that sort of thing. You roll underneath it, which gives you more than enough cover, and decide to fall asleep where you are.

You are awoken a few hours later by a thunderous yell from upstairs. "LITTLE MAN?" came the voice and you winced. Tim was looking for you! You could hear stuff in the bedroom being moved around as Tim searched frantically for you. You just stayed still, wincing as each fresh object hit the floor, as Tim stomped around. "Son of a bitch!" Tim yelled aloud. "Where did he go?"

Eventually, the noise stopped and you heard a huge sigh from the bedroom. Then, the footstops grew louder and more intense as Tim thundered down the steps.
155332211111112.title: Try and slip out

choices: No, but he leaves - you're stuck in the house!
Yes, he lifts it up and spots you!
1553322111111121.content:
You cower under the shoe-rack as you see the huge, bare feet hit the ground at the bottom of the steps. You wince as the ground shakes angrily beneath your feet as Tim begins searching through the house, looking for you.

But does he check under the shoe-rack?
1553322111111121.title: Tim keeps looking for you downstairs

choices: Yes
No
1553322111111122.content:
Tim stomped around angrily, wearing those very same sneakers he'd put you in to sleep. pissed off you'd gone but - as far as you could tell, anyway - he was no longer searching for you. Perhaps he figured you were too small to be found. Anyway, after a few minutes of the ground shaking as he walked, you saw the huge feet pounding towards the shoe rack. You blanched in fear, scared of being found...but he walked right past the rack to the door. You could see his feet now, standing at the entrance of the house, the impassable door.

"Wallet...keys..." he muttered to himself. He was checking he had everything...now or never!

Taking a deep breath, you sprinted out from the cover of the shoe-rack. One of the white laces of the sneaker was dangling just within reach and, straining, you hopped onto it. Just in time, too, as Tim opened the door and stepped out...

You stifled a yell and tried not to look around too much as the world around you devolved into utter chaos. The two steps the coach took were utterly devastating, weapons of mass destruction that shook everything around them. You were in awe at his incredible, effortless power.

Then you felt the fresh air on your face and jolted with the impact as Tim stepped onto his gravel drive. Lucky you were invincible! You smiled - you'd made it!

But had Tim felt something on the sneaker?
1553322111111122.title: Tim gets ready to leave for work - time to escape!

choices: Tim sees straight through your bullshit.
Tim believes you. What now?
15533221111111221.content:
Clearly the extra little bit of weight on the sneaker had been enough for Tim to realize something was wrong, because as soon as Tim landed on the drive he stopped walking. "Huh?" he said, and looked down, all the way down. And you looked up, all the way up, into his beautiful yet terrifying visage.

"Little guy?" he said, a look of disbelief on his face. Your guts nearly emptied themselves in terror as Coach Dantry as the foot you had grabbed suddenly shook, loosening your grip until you fell loose of the sneaker. You yelled out as you smacked the ground hard, your life raft no longer in your grip.

Now the air around you swirled and whooshed, completely disorienting you as the tracksuited, godly coach squatted down, legs on either side of you and crotch exposed. It would have been breathtaking if you weren't so scared. You simply stared up at him, with no idea of how to react. His face was hard to read, displaying what looked to be a mix of emotions - anger, amusement, relief... "How the hell did you get there?" he said, almost laughing.

You decided to make something up. The truth - you were trying to escape him because you were scared he might abuse you - probably wouldn't go over too well. You took a deep breath and yelled so he'd hear you. "I couldn't sleep, so I thought I'd look around...then I took a nap, and the next thing I know you were leaving! So I decided to grab your shoe! I did try to get your attention but you didn't hear me."

On the fly, it was a decent enough lie, you thought. But would Tim believe it?
15533221111111221.title: Yes

choices: Tim is sad you didn't trust him - he has nothing but good intentions for you.
Tim laughs and takes control.
155332211111112211.content:
Tim exhaled through his nose aggressively, scoffing at what you'd said. Not a good sign.

Out of nowhere came the enormous hand, trapping you in the fist once again as you went on a wild, but brief, ride. You heard the slamming of Tim's car door and soon you were being let out of the hand...on a car seat. You looked ahead and saw the steering wheel...and on either side, two black-tracksuited legs. You gulped and turned. Tim's crotch was right in front of you and up above was his downturned face, which was staring at you with an angry expression.

"You think I'm going to fall for that bullshit?" he asked. "I know you were trying to escape, little man."
155332211111112211.title: Tim sees straight through your bullshit.

choices: Look around for someone you know
Enter the building
15533221111111222.content:
Tim walked on, unaware that you'd hitched a ride. He grumbled repeatedly to himself as he walked, clearly angry that you'd slipped out of his grasp. You realized you'd made the right decision - his anger and frustration indicated he probably had plans for you which would have been far from pleasant.

Before you knew it he'd climbed into the car, with you swinging around wildly on a shoelace you only just had a grip of. Only when he finally sat down and planted his foot hard on the floor did you allow yourself to drop off. You scurried under the seat and listened as Tim put the radio on and began to drive, quickly, aggressively and angrily.

You stared at the strong, muscular calves ahead of you from the seat and sighed. If only you could trust him.

After 10-15 minutes, Tim arrived at the college. You realized you would have to act fast - you ran forward and grabbed the laces just as he opened the door and made to climb out. The centrifugal force whipped you all over the place but you maintained a strong grip until the wrestling coach hit the ground. At that point, keen not to ride your luck any further, you let go.

So now what?
15533221111111222.title: No

15533221111112.content:
Just as you make it half way across the bedroom towards the door leading to the hallway, the snoring stops and you hear rustling in the bed. You slide to a step and turn around. With just enough moonlight shining through the curtain, you see Tim slowly sit up and swing his feet over the side of the bed.

*boom*

His perfectly shaped, manly bare feet landed firmly on the floor as he stands up. You panic as he stands up and begins to walk towards the door, which just happens to be in your path.

*Boom* *Boom*

You are too afraid to move as Tim towers over you as he casually walks. "TIM!! SIR I'M DOWN HERE!!" You scream up to him.
